mechanism_narrative: A sustained period of low and stable federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces the cost of capital and supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with low FEDFUNDS levels over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, which negatively impacts corporate earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where the UNRATE trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with strong housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand, driving corporate revenues and supporting equity prices over the near term.
failure_condition: The joint condition of strong RSAFS and HOUST does not lead to significantly higher SPX 20-day returns compared to periods where only one or neither is strong (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of already low industrial production (INDPRO), any further decline signals a deepening manufacturing recession, which negatively impacts corporate profits and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances when INDPRO declines during a low INDPRO regime.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, prompting central bank tightening and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds the 4% trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent and positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, translating to positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A significant slowdown in GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates a broad economic contraction, signaling reduced corporate profitability and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 1% AND HOUST 3-month average < 1000
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where both GDPC1 growth is low and HOUST is declining.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment, a decrease in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an attempt by the central bank to stimulate the economy, which can lead to a recovery in equity markets as liquidity improves and future growth expectations rise.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS decreases during a high unemployment regime.
